items.title: Malaysia’s private hospitals report no disruption to medicine supply despite West Asia war, says APHM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Medicine supply in private hospitals across the country remains stable amid the war in West Asia, the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) said today. The association said the country’s pharmaceutical supply chain has not reported any indications of being directly affected by the ongoing conflict. However, the association said it will closely monitor the developments in the region and the subsequent impact on the cost of medicines and medical consumables.

items.title: Serdang deputy police chief dies of heart attack at 51
items.summary: SERDANG: Serdang deputy police chief Superintendent Shahnaz Akhtar Haji has died. He is understood to have suffered a heart attack and died at Serdang Hospital about 10am. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed the news.

items.title: Police recover four urns, record 10 statements in Nilai urn theft case
items.summary: NILAI: Police have recorded statements from 10 witnesses and recovered four urns containing human ashes as investigations continue into the theft that has shocked families and the community. Nilai police chief Superintendent Johari Yahya said the probe was ongoing, with investigators working to complete witness statements and verify the identities of the ashes in the recovered urns. * 30 urns from the Xiao En Memorial Park in Nilai, Negri Sembilan on March 18.

items.title: Appeals Court trims jail term of man who murdered senior citizen
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: An Indian national sentenced to 38 years in prison by the Shah Alam High Court for fatally hammering a man at a senior citizens' home in 2020 has had his sentence reduced by three years by the Court of Appeal (CoA). A three-judge panel led by Datuk Azman Abdullah upheld the High Court's conviction of Alagesan Kamaraj but allowed his appeal on sentence. "We therefore impose a sentence of 35 years imprisonment with 12 strokes of whipping," he said.

items.title: On Police Day, IGP says PDRM’s 219-year legacy shows unwavering commitment to national security
items.summary: =KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) remains a steadfast bulwark in preserving national security and stability, with more than two centuries of history and sacrifice. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said that behind the blue uniform worn by officers lie countless stories of courage, sacrifice and selflessness, given without expectation of reward. In the same post, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay, PERKEP Malaysia chairman Datin Seri Datuk Dr Munirah Che Rose, and vice-chairman Datuk Zalena Mohd Ariffin also conveyed their appreciation in conjunction with Police Day celebrations.

items.title: Lorry driver detained after 'reckless' driving chase in Kulim
items.summary: KULIM: A 27-year-old lorry driver with a prior drug offence record has been arrested following a high-speed police chase in Padang Serai. He drove recklessly, crashed through a roadside tent and tested positive for methamphetamine. Police, however, said the suspect was apprehended at about 8pm yesterday during a raid at Kampung Sidam Kanan.

items.title: MMEA rescues five Thai crew after cargo vessel fire off Johor coast
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Five Thai crew members were rescued after a cargo vessel caught fire in its galley off the Johor coast yesterday. Acting Tanjung Sedili Maritime Zone director, Maritime Commander Mohd Najib Sam, said the incident occurred about 30 nautical miles east of Tanjung Sedili at around 10.20am. The Johor Bahru Maritime Rescue Sub Centre (MRSC) received a distress call at 12.20pm and deployed the Pekasa 30 boat to carry out a medical evacuation (medevac) operation.

items.title: Seven-year-old boy dies after car hits him while crossing road in Kota Kuala Muda
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 25 — A seven-year-old boy was killed after being hit by a car while crossing the road along Jalan Kota Kuala Muda near here last night. Muhammad Ammar Zamri, a pupil of Sekolah Kebangsaan Kota Kuala Muda, was believed to have suddenly crossed the road before being struck by a Perodua Myvi. Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the incident occurred at 9.23pm, with police receiving a report at 9.45pm.

items.title: DBKL fallen tree suit: Routine maintenance not a choice, but life-saving responsibility
items.summary: While planting trees and beautifying cities are commendable initiatives, these efforts must be accompanied by consistent inspection, risk assessment, and timely maintenance. This responsibility extends beyond trees. Public safety must never be left to chance — proper maintenance saves lives.

items.title: Penang man's RON95 purchase in viral video is legal, says KPDN
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) confirmed that no offence was committed in a viral incident showing a man filling RON95 petrol into a plastic container at a petrol station in Bayan Baru. Jegan said any purchase exceeding 20 litres into containers required a special controlled goods permit issued by the ministry. A video went viral yesterday showing a man filling a plastic container with RON95 petrol at a station on Jalan Tengah, Bayan Baru.

items.title: It's go time: Historic Moon mission set for lift-off
items.summary: WASHINGTON: More than half-a-century after the groundbreaking Apollo programme's last crewed flight to the Moon, three men and one woman are preparing for a lunar journey set to turn a new page in American space exploration. It's also the inaugural crewed flight of NASA's new lunar rocket, dubbed SLS. - Space Race 2.0?

items.title: From ‘agak‑agak’ to ‘Malaysia boleh’: Oxford English Dictionary adds new Malaysia and Singapore words in latest update
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Oxford English Dictionary (OED) has added several Malaysian and Singaporean English terms in its latest update, highlighting the region’s linguistic influence on global English. Oxford Languages announced the additions in a press release yesterday, noting that many of the new entries originate from Malay and Hokkien. BTO , n. BTO , v. degazette , v. ice kacang , n. jialat , adj.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution commends police for being ever-ready to safeguard the country
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The role of a police officer is more than just a profession; it is a solemn responsibility to the nation, said Minister of Home Affairs Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil also extended his heartfelt greetings, expressing his appreciation for the police, particularly as they continue to carry out their duties. Happy Police Day!” he wrote on Facebook.

items.title: Teen dies after fall from seventh floor of apartment building
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A teenage boy died yesterday after reportedly falling from the seventh floor of an apartment building in Section 19, Shah Alam. Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Ramsay Embol said police received a report at 6.44pm yesterday. A post-mortem will be conducted today at Shah Alam Hospital to determine the cause of death.

items.title: Fire at Kuwait airport after drones hit fuel tank
items.summary: KUWAIT CITY: Drones hit a fuel tank and sparked a fire at Kuwait International Airport, the Gulf state's civil aviation authority said on Wednesday, as Iran presses on with its attacks in the nearly four-week regional war. The Kuwait National Guard meanwhile said in a statement that its forces intercepted six drones early Wednesday, and the army said air defences were "responding to hostile missile and drone attacks." On March 14, the civil aviation authority said an attack with "several drones" targeted the airport and "struck its radar system."

items.title: MMEA rescues five Thai sailors from burning cargo ship off Kota Tinggi
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 25 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) rescued five Thai crew members after their cargo ship caught fire about 30 nautical miles east of Tanjung Sedili here yesterday. MMEA Tanjung Sedili maritime zone director Maritime Commander Mohd Najib Sam said the Johor Bahru Maritime Rescue Sub Centre (MRSC) received a distress call in the incident at 12.20pm. “All five male crew members, aged between 25 and 61, were rescued via a medical evacuation (medevac) operation.

items.title: Trial run of Jalan Genting Highlands road charge system to start April 2, no payment required
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Lingkaran Cekap Sdn Bhd (LCSB), the authorised operator for Jalan Genting Highlands, will begin a trial run of its road charge system on April 2, ahead of full implementation. The test will be conducted at two locations: the Genting Sempah entry to Genting Highlands and the Gohtong Jaya entry at the Gohtong Jaya roundabout. Late last year, Genting Malaysia confirmed plans to introduce a private vehicle charge on Jalan Genting Highlands based on a “user pays” principle to support ongoing maintenance and emergency response needs.

items.title: Trump sends peace plan as Iran opens to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
items.summary: TEHRAN: US President Donald Trump sent a peace plan to Iran as he voiced optimism Tuesday at ending nearly a month of warfare, with Tehran announcing that it will let "non-hostile" oil vessels go through the crucial Strait of Hormuz. Trump, whose pronouncements in recent days have swung wildly from vowing massive attacks on Iran to declaring the nearly month-long war virtually over, said the United States was "in negotiations right now" with Iran – which has not confirmed any formal talks. - New nuclear deal?

items.title: Dialog, Velesto gain momentum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dialog Bhd and Velesto Energy Bhd are drawing renewed investor interest as both counters flash bullish technical signals, pointing to a potential extension of their near-term uptrends, RHB Research said. Dialog, in particular, is showing signs of extending its upward momentum after staging a convincing bullish breakout, signalling a shift in near-term market sentiment. The research house noted that Dialog formed a long bullish candlestick and closed decisively above the RM2.05 resistance level, a technical development that points to strengthening buying interest and accelerating upward momentum.

items.title: Woman wails during Tangkak roadside confrontation [WATCH]
items.summary: TANGKAK: A heated roadside confrontation erupted in Tangkak, Johor, as two men shouted at each other while bystanders restrained them and a woman wailed continuously. Police are aware of the incident, an official told the New Straits Times. At one point, a man is heard shouting, "this is my wife," while another challenges him, saying, "let's go one on one."

items.title: Boy, 7, killed in Sungai Petani road accident
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A seven-year-old boy was killed after being hit by a car while crossing a road at Jalan Kota Kuala Muda here. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police received information at 9.45pm on an accident involving a car that struck a pedestrian at Kota Kuala Muda. "The driver was travelling from Kota Kuala Muda towards Pulau Sayak when the child suddenly crossed from the left into the path of the vehicle.

items.title: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT to buy industrial, data centre assets for US$1.1bil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore's CapitaLand Ascendas REIT said on Tuesday it will but interests in three assets in Singapore and Japan for S$1.41 billion (US$1.1 billion), including fees and expenses, as it aims to bolster its income base and diversify its portfolio. Here are some details: * The acquisitions include a 100 per cent stake in 25 Loyang Crescent, a logistics and industrial asset in Singapore, for S$504.2 million, and a 50 per cent stake in Ascent, a business space property, for S$245 million. * A global sovereign wealth fund will own the remaining 50 per cent of Ascent.

items.title: Europe hit by prime office supply crunch as construction slumps
items.summary: LONDON: Construction of new office space in Europe has slumped to its lowest in a decade, even as rents in prime locations hit record highs, research shows, deepening a supply crunch that is forcing many occupants to stay put. However, space under construction fell to 10.1 million square feet at the end of last year, the lowest level since 2016, the report said, citing the impact of high construction and financing costs. Demand for new office space in London is at over 11 million square feet, about 20 per cent above the long-term average, according to separate research from agency Knight Frank, which also predicted a looming supply crunch.

items.title: Hot weather in Malaysia likely to persist until June, warns MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The hot weather phenomenon currently affecting the country is expected to continue until the beginning of the Southwest Monsoon (MBD) in June, following drier atmospheric conditions and lower rainfall, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). MetMalaysia deputy director-general (Operations) Ambun Dindang said Malaysia is currently in the final phase of the Northeast Monsoon (MTL) which is usually characterised by reduced rainfall and hotter and drier weather conditions, especially in the northern and interior areas of the Peninsula. He said based on local and international weather model projections, the country is expected to experience drier weather patterns with lower rainfall than usual for the next few months.

items.title: Firecracker shop owner detained after blaze in Kota Baru
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The owner of a firecracker premises that caught fire in Wakaf Che Yeh yesterday has been detained for further investigation. The 44-year-old woman, who also owns the eatery, was detained along with her 38-year-old employee at 8.30pm last night. "At 8.30pm last night, police arrested two women, including the premises owner, for investigation under Section 285 of the Penal Code," he said, according to Harian Metro.

items.title: Empire Sushi chain owner aims for RM254mil in Bursa debut at 70 sen IPO
items.summary: The IPO comprises 218 million issue shares, alongside 145 million existing shares offered for sale, bringing the total offering to 363 million shares. A total of 293 million shares, including 145 million offer shares and 148 million issue shares, will be allocated for institutional investors, while 137.5 million shares are reserved for Bumiputera investors under approval from MITI. The remaining 70 million shares comprise 55 million shares open to the Malaysian public, of which 27.5 million are reserved for Bumiputera investors and 15 million shares are set aside for applications by eligible company personnel.

items.title: King extends Police Day greetings, honours PDRM personnel for dedication
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia today conveyed his greetings in conjunction with the 219th Police Day to all personnel and veterans of the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). Sultan Ibrahim said the commitment shown by PDRM personnel, including during festive seasons and emergency situations, is highly valued as they form one of the main pillars of national security. “May Allah Subhanahu Wa Ta’ala always protect all members of the police force in every duty they undertake.

items.title: Quadruple amputee charged with murder after shooting passenger in car
items.summary: Dayton Webber, 27, a professional American Cornhole League athlete, is accused of killing Bradrick Wells, 27, following a dispute on Sunday night in La Plata, Maryland, The Guardian reported. Police said witnesses seated in the back of the car told investigators that Webber, who was driving, opened fire and shot the front-seat passenger during the argument. The Charles County Sheriff's Office in a statement said Webber later pulled over and asked the back-seat passengers to help remove the victim from the car.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM MARCH 25, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened firmer on Wednesday at 1,722, buoyed by improved risk appetite. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin is trading around RM279,609 and Ethereum climbed to RM8,541. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 'Hero' Australian dog who saved 100 koalas retires
items.summary: SYDNEY: An Australian dog credited with saving over 100 koalas from bushfires is retiring after a decade of service. Bear, an 11-year-old Australian Koolie, was one of the first dogs in the country to be trained on the scent of koala fur. The International Fund for Animal Welfare called using dogs to detect koalas a "novel" approach.

items.title: Major highways nationwide hit by slow traffic, accidents and breakdowns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Traffic on several major highways nationwide was slow as of this morning due to increased vehicle volume, as well as accidents and breakdowns. Meanwhile, traffic entering Malaysia from Singapore via the Johor Causeway was also slow, from the Woodlands Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex to the Sultan Iskandar CIQ in Johor Bahru. Slow-moving traffic was also reported on the Damansara-Puchong Expressway (LDP) northbound from Puchong Utama to Puchong Intan.

items.title: Eversendai slides on project termination
items.summary: The stock opened at 32 sen, below its previous close of 35.5 sen, and continued to decline, dropping 22.54 per cent or 8 sen to 27.5 sen at the time of writing. Eversendai said the termination of its contract for the Neom Trojena Ski Village project was likely linked to the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The company noted that its current order book stands at RM2.02 billion, excluding the remaining works under the Trojena Ski Village contract, while its tender book totals RM18.4 billion.

items.title: Currency markets drift as traders sceptical of US efforts to end Iran war
items.summary: The euro edged higher, up 0.1 per cent at US$1.1619, with most other currency pairs remaining unchanged. The British pound was up 0.1 per cent at US$1.3428, while the New Zealand dollar was flat at US$0.5834. In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin climbed 1.2 per cent to US$70,910.16 while ether was up 0.8 per cent at US$2,164.74.

items.title: Press Metal gains on aluminium upswing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd is expected to benefit from elevated aluminium prices driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB Research). HLIB Research raised the group's financial year 2026 and 2027 (FY26/FY27) earnings forecasts by 13 per cent and 18.7 per cent, respectively, driven by higher London Metal Exchange (LME) aluminium price assumptions, revised hedging positions and stronger Main Japanese Port (MJP) premiums. HLIB Research said the escalation of the US-Iran conflict has pushed LME aluminium prices above US$3,400 per metric tonne, raising the likelihood of the global market shifting from a modest surplus in 2025 to a deficit in 2026 due to supply disruptions.

items.title: Water supply disruption dampens Hari Raya festivities for residents of Gua Musang
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Water cuts have hit Gua Musang, disrupting Hari Raya festivities as residents in Tanah Putih face prolonged clean water shortages. "I received many complaints about clean water supply problems, especially as Aidilfitri approached," he said. Mohd Syahbuddin said water tankers provided only short-term relief and did not resolve the clean water supply issue.

items.title: Shah Alam teen dies after suspected fall from seventh-floor apartment
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 25 — A 14-year-old boy died after he is believed to have fallen from the seventh floor of an apartment in Section 19 here yesterday afternoon. He was found unconscious by members of the public at the base of the building around 4.20 pm and was rushed to Hospital Shah Alam, where he was pronounced dead at 5.40 pm. Shah Alam police chief ACP Ramsay Embol said a report on the incident was received at 6.44 pm.

items.title: 7 foreigners denied entry at Bukit Kayu Hitam over invalid tickets, dubious claims
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM: The Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) here yesterday denied entry to seven foreigners, including six Indian nationals, after they failed to provide a credible purpose of travel and were found with invalid return flight tickets. Bukit Kayu Hitam MCBA commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohd Nasarudin M Nasir said the group of Indian nationals also tried to mislead Immigration officers by claiming they did not know one another, but checks revealed they held flight tickets with identical details. "All were denied entry after failing to provide a credible purpose of travel and for possessing invalid return tickets," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Kim vows North Korea's 'unshakable' support for Russia
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un said his country will always support Russia, state media reported on Wednesday, in a thankyou letter to his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin. Ties between the two have grown closer since Moscow's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, with Pyongyang dispatching ground troops and weapons systems to aid Russia's war effort. North Korea's rubber-stamp legislature reappointed Kim this week as head of the authoritarian nation's highest policymaking and governing body.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia opens higher on improved regional risk appetite
items.summary: The key index opened 13.74 points higher at 1,722.50. Among heavyweight counters, Maybank added 18 sen to RM11.52, Public Bank rose nine sen to RM4.95, CIMB and IHH Healthcare gained eight sen to RM7.91 and RM9.06, while Tenaga Nasional rose six sen to RM14.34. On the most active list, Sunway Healthcare topped up 10 sen to RM2.10, AirAsia X was four sen higher at RM1.21, Eversendai fell nine sen to 26.5 sen, Borneo Oil was flat at half a sen, and Capital A increased half a sen to 41 sen.

items.title: Four-hectare forest fire in Kota Bharu brought under control, no casualties reported
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 25 — A forest fire covering approximately four hectares in Kampung Lati here was successfully brought under control by the Fire and Rescue Department without any loss of life yesterday afternoon. Tunjong Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) operations commander Azhar Mohamed said the department received an emergency call at 3.20 pm before personnel arrived at the scene with assistance from a water tanker from the Rantau Panjang BBP. “The Fire and Rescue Department managed to save several houses located near the fire from being affected.

items.title: Education Ministry to ease teachers’ workload under Malaysia Education Blueprint 2026–2035
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Education Ministry (MOE) remains committed to implementing various initiatives to address teachers’ workload, as outlined in the Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the RPM 2026–2035 sets priorities to ensure teachers can fully focus on teaching and learning (PdP). Since 2023, various initiatives have been implemented to reduce teachers’ workload, including appointing 20,997 volunteers for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examinations, implementing the Malaysia Short-Term Employment Programme (MySTEP) for teacher assistants, and appointing full-time hostel wardens’ assistants.

items.title: Three held as machete-wielding riot in Teluk Panglima Garang goes viral
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 25 — Police have arrested three men to assist in investigations into a rioting incident at Jalan Waja, Teluk Panglima Garang, Kuala Langat, on Tuesday. Acting Kuala Langat district police chief DSP Mohd Sufian Amin said the suspects, aged between 39 and 45, were detained following a police report lodged at 2.51 pm by a 38-year-old man. “Following investigations and intelligence gathering, three men believed to be involved were arrested, and several case-related items were seized,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Anwar urges peaceful resolution to West Asia tensions after calls with Bahrain, UAE leaders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Malaysia maintains its stance that all disputes in West Asia must be resolved through peaceful means to ease escalating tensions in the region, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Apart from discussing regional security issues, Anwar said he and the two leaders also exchanged Aidilfitri greetings and expressed hope for the well-being, peace and prosperity of their respective nations. Regional tensions have continued to escalate since the United States (US) and Israel launched joint attacks on Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Eight-year-old boy found drowned in Sungai Muda in Kedah
items.summary: SIK, March 25 — An eight-year-old boy was found drowned in Sungai Muda in Kampung Tanjung Jeneri near here, today. Sik District Police deputy chief DSP Safuan Md Noor said the victim, Muhammad Fatah Saiful, a pupil of SK Kota Bukit near here, had left his house at about 2 pm to cycle with some friends. The victim was said to have gone to the river at 4.30 pm with his friends, and was believed to have drowned shortly after jumping into the water.

items.title: Tomb raiders: From Hong Kong to Malaysia, urn thefts over the years
items.summary: This contrasts with the urns stolen from Xiao En Memorial Park and Nirvana, where the thieves demanded ransoms. The difference is that, in Nirvana's case, the urns were recovered. Missing urn and an email address In 2014, a family was told to pay 600,000 yuan for the return of an urn stolen from its family plot.

items.title: Malaysia may face hottest El Niño on record
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A climatology and climate change expert expects this year to record the world's highest temperatures, driven by the El Niño phenomenon and the effects of global warming. Climatology and climate change expert Professor Emeritus Dr Fredolin Tangang said the El Niño phase has the potential to raise global temperatures, noting that 2024 was declared the hottest year on record following a 1.55 degrees celsius increase. El Niño refers to a condition of extreme environmental warming, where sea surface temperatures rise above average.

items.title: Nearly 30,000 eels lost — but this Kelantan duo turned failure into a farming win
items.summary: TUMPAT, March 25 — Two friends who embarked on an eel farming venture five years ago initially suffered huge losses when nearly 30,000 eels died due to disease. Muhammad Azman Abd Rashid @ Ramli and Muhamad Hairi Ishak today run a thriving eel farm on a 0.4-hectare site in Kampung Banggol Petani here, having shifted from farming wild eels to hybrid species. “I have loved eels since I was a child.

items.title: Can locally grown/produced food cushion Malaysia from global shocks caused by the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: While the country is unlikely to face immediate shortages, the bigger impact lies in rising costs, as Malaysia remains reliant on global markets for key staples and agricultural inputs that underpin even locally produced food. However, Malaysia is also known for its local produce. What food is actually ‘local’?

items.title: From sunburn to stroke — experts warn heatwave packs more than discomfort
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The heatwave currently affecting the country not only increases health risks but may also lead to long-term physiological complications such as heatstroke. Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) public health medicine expert, Associate Prof Dr Malina Osman said health risks from hot weather can be divided into two main categories: acute effects and long-term consequences. “This condition typically affects individuals unaccustomed to heat or those suddenly exposed to high temperatures.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu: Malaysia steps up food security measures as West Asia conflict and hot weather bite
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM), together with relevant agencies, has stepped up preparedness efforts to safeguard the nation’s food security amid ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia and the ongoing hot weather. He said that uncertainties in the global landscape resulting from the geopolitical conflict in West Asia could affect the global supply chain with higher oil prices and logistics costs ultimately impacting food prices. Therefore, Mohamad assured that essential food supplies for this year’s Aidilfitri celebration would remain stable, sufficient, and accessible at competitive prices.

items.title: Hornbill crashes into building as bush fire haze blankets Miri, firefighters race to save protected bird
items.summary: MIRI, March 24 — The Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) rescued a critically injured hornbill found on the ground near a building in the Kuala Baram Industrial Estate, as Miri experienced sweltering heat and bush fires in Lutong on Tuesday. The distressed bird, a protected species, was discovered by members of the public near Bangunan Daiken Miri Sdn Bhd, who promptly alerted Bomba. Bomba Miri personnel responded to the call and found the hornbill in a weakened state and sent it to Piasau Nature Reserve (PNR) at Pulau Melayu for further treatment and care, said Bomba Lutong Station chief Henry Jugah.

items.title: Student facing court charges over Quran desecration and offensive Threads posts expelled from university
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 24 — A student at Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah (UMPSA), charged with desecrating the Quran and posting offensive content on social media, has been expelled from the university. UMPSA vice-chancellor, Prof Dr Yatimah Alias, said the male student’s disciplinary case was recently closed after the Student Disciplinary Committee met on March 9. “Following disciplinary proceedings, the student was found guilty and expelled under Rule 58 of the Universiti and University College (UMPSA) (Student Discipline) Rules 2024,” she said in a statement today.

items.title: Aidilfitri amid the flames: Kota Tinggi firefighters battle 150-hectare blaze through festive season
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 24 — While millions across the country are celebrating Aidilfitri, the orange-clad heroes from the Punggai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) here are spending the festive season amid intense heat and thick plumes of smoke. Since March 21, they have been working tirelessly to extinguish a 150-hectare bush fire along the Punggai Bypass Highway heading towards Sebana Cove, which is now entering its fourth day. For auxiliary fire officer Iz’aan Nazmi Suri, 24, this year’s celebration carries special meaning as he recently got married in January.

items.title: Highway jams stretch north, south and east as Klang Valley-bound motorists make their way home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Traffic flow on several major expressways nationwide showed an increasing trend, with slow-moving traffic reported following a rise in the number of vehicles heading towards the Klang Valley as of 4pm today. Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) Traffic Management Centre supervisor Muhammad Aliff Syafiq Md Takiyudin said congestion was detected on routes heading north, south and the east coast. Commenting on traffic disruptions, Muhammad Aliff Syafiq said several incidents involving broken-down vehicles had contributed to congestion, including in the northern sector after the Menora Tunnel, heading towards the city centre, which caused a traffic build-up of about seven kilometres (km).

items.title: No shortage here: Armizan says petrol, diesel and LPG supply steady as authorities step up monitoring
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — The current supply of petrol, diesel and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) in the country remains stable, with no shortages reported so far, says Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali. “These issues have been addressed, and continuous monitoring is being carried out to ensure smooth delivery of supplies,” he said in a Facebook post today after paying a courtesy call on Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said claims of insufficient fuel supply at several petrol stations in Sabah recently were also being linked to supply disruption situations in neighbouring countries, and therefore urged the public not to engage in panic buying, as supplies were sufficient.

items.title: Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband’s Budi95 petrol subsidy account still active; lawyers ask why not suspended
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Lawyers for Hindu mother M. Indira Gandhi highlighted that the Budi95 petrol subsidy account of her ex-husband K. Patmanathan is still active even until today, even as he remains a wanted man after he took their daughter away. Indira’s lawyers Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh said it is publicly known that the Malaysian authorities have been aware since November 2025 that Patmanathan has been and continues to be eligible for the Budi95 subsidy. Lawyers for Hindu mother M. Indira Gandhi highlighted that the Budi95 petrol subsidy account of her ex-husband K. Patmanathan is still active.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s local currency trade with China, Thailand and Indonesia surges to RM82b, cutting reliance on US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia recorded RM82.1 billion in trade and investment payments with China, Thailand and Indonesia settled in local currencies as at November 2025, a sharp rise from just RM3.6 billion in 2009. Anwar said Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) has taken proactive steps to promote local currency use in cross-border trade, reducing exposure to exchange rate volatility and strengthening financial resilience. “The government and BNM will continue to strengthen and diversify local currency settlement channels to facilitate trade and investment, supporting the sustainability of the regional financial system,” Anwar said.

items.title: Heatwave fails to slow Penang’s holiday buzz as tourists flock to heritage streets and seaside spots
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — Scorching temperatures in Penang, particularly in the Timur Laut district, have done little to deter tourists, with popular attractions continuing to draw strong crowds during the Hari Raya and school holiday period. Despite the hot weather, these locations remained lively, with tourists strolling, taking photos and enjoying their time in Penang. Wang Fang, 30, from China, said the warm weather had not affected her travel experience, describing Penang as a tourist-friendly destination.

items.title: Fun in the sun? Perlis at 40°C reshapes 2026 Aidilfitri celebrations for Chuping residents
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, March 24 — The searing heat in Chuping, Perlis has locals staying home and avoiding the midday sun, choosing to go out only for the essentials instead of visiting each others’ homes for open house parties this festive season. With temperatures rising to 40°Celsius, celebrations this Raya Puasa are hitting residents hard, Berita Harian reported today. Housewife Nur Hafsah Awalluddin, 39, said the extreme weather forced her to take extra care for her children.

items.title: MMEA swoops in off Lumut, detains two tankers over suspected illegal oil transfer near Pulau Jarak
items.summary: IPOH, March 24 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained two oil tankers suspected of conducting unauthorised ship-to-ship (STS) bunkering activities near Pulau Jarak, Lumut, yesterday. Its Perak director, Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob, said the vessels were intercepted at 8pm, some 11 nautical miles east of the island. He said both vessels were ordered to anchor 11.5 nautical miles east of Pulau Jarak for handover to investigation officers, with monitoring assisted by the Lumut Sea Surveillance System (Swasla).
